enforced.mr_name: CIGAR-SEQ length consistency violation must cause rejection
enforced.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject (raise error, crash, or refuse to emit) a SAM record where sum of query-consuming CIGAR operators (M, I, S, =, X) does not equal SEQ length when SEQ != '*'. Silent acceptance or silent record-drop is a conformance bug.

evidence.quote: SEQ: segment SEQuence. This field can be a `*' when the sequence is not stored. If not a `*', the length of the sequence must equal the sum of lengths of MIS=X operations in CIGAR.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM header TAG:VALUE pair ordering invariance
quarantine.oracle: Parsed SAM header metadata must be semantically equivalent regardless of intra-line TAG:VALUE pair ordering

evidence.quote: Within each (non- @CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.
Within each (non- @CO) header line, no field tag may appear more than once and the order in which the fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM optional tag field ordering invariance
quarantine.oracle: Parsed SAM alignment records must be semantically equivalent regardless of optional TAG:TYPE:VALUE field ordering

evidence.quote: Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.
Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM header TAG:VALUE pair permutation invariance
quarantine.oracle: The parsed header must be semantically equivalent after shuffling TAG:VALUE pairs within @SQ lines

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p133
evidence.quote: * When a PG tag appears anywhere in the alignment section, there should be a single corresponding @PG line with matching ID tag in the header.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: The evidence is ADVISORY severity; the normative rule about TAG:VALUE ordering being insignificant is in chunk SAMv1.tex::The SAM Format Specification::p26 but above threshold

quarantine.mr_name: SAM CIGAR operation split/merge invariance
quarantine.oracle: The parsed alignment must be semantically equivalent after splitting or merging adjacent identical CIGAR operations

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p134
evidence.quote: * Adjacent CIGAR operations should be different.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: The spec uses 'should' (ADVISORY) not 'must', so parsers MAY treat adjacent identical operations differently but SHOULD normalize them

quarantine.mr_name: Flag-bit exclusivity violation must cause rejection
quarantine.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject (raise error, crash, or refuse to emit) a SAM record where flag 0x4 (segment unmapped) is set while RNAME != '*' and POS > 0. The spec states that when 0x4 is set, no assumptions can be made about RNAME, POS, CIGAR, MAPQ, and bits 0x2, 0x100, and 0x800, implying these fields should be ignored or set to placeholders.

evidence.quote: Bit 0x4 is the only reliable place to tell whether the read is unmapped. If 0x4 is set, no assumptions can be made about RNAME, POS, CIGAR, MAPQ, and bits 0x2, 0x100, and 0x800.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p137
evidence.quote: If all segments in a template are unmapped, their RNAME should be set as `*' and POS as 0.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

ambiguity_flags: The 'should' in the second quote indicates advisory practice, but the first quote establishes that when 0x4 is set, RNAME and POS cannot be trusted for mapping information.

quarantine.mr_name: Optional tag type character violation must cause rejection
quarantine.oracle: A compliant parser MUST reject (raise error, crash, or refuse to emit) a SAM record where an optional tag's TYPE character is not one of the permitted set (A, i, f, Z, H, B). The spec defines TYPE as a single case-sensitive letter that defines the format of VALUE.

evidence.quote: All optional fields follow the TAG:TYPE:VALUE format where TAG is a two-character string that matches /[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9]/. Within each alignment line, no TAG may appear more than once and the order in which the optional fields appear is not significant. A TAG containing lowercase letters is reserved for end users. In an optional field, TYPE is a single case-sensitive letter which defines the format of VALUE:

ambiguity_flags: The spec doesn't explicitly list all valid TYPE characters in this chunk, but the SAMtags specification (referenced) defines A, i, f, Z, H, B as the valid types.

quarantine.mr_name: Coordinate system invariance under SAM-BAM roundtrip
quarantine.oracle: POS field values must remain identical after SAM→BAM→SAM conversion, preserving 1-based coordinate semantics

evidence.quote: [SAM v1 — The SAM Format Specification] [1-based coordinate system] A coordinate system where the first base of a sequence is one. In this coordinate system, a region is specified by a closed interval. For example, the region between the 3rd and the 7th bases inclusive is $[3,7]$. The SAM, VCF, GFF and Wiggle formats are using the 1-based coordinate system.

evidence.chunk_id: SAMv1.tex::Recommended Practice for the SAM Format::p137
evidence.quote: [SAM v1 — Recommended Practice for the SAM Format] * If all segments in a template are unmapped, their RNAME should be set as `*' and POS as 0.
evidence.section_id: Recommended Practice for the SAM Format

quarantine.mr_name: Hard/soft clipping equivalence
quarantine.oracle: Alignment with hard clipping (H) converted to soft clipping (S) with appropriate SEQ/QUAL padding must represent the same biological alignment

evidence.quote: S and N are the soft-clipping and reference-clip CIGAR operators, respectively

ambiguity_flags: The spec doesn't explicitly state equivalence between H and S clipping representations, though both represent unaligned segments

quarantine.mr_name: TLEN sign consistency violation rejection
quarantine.oracle: All SUTs must REJECT (error/crash) or at minimum agree on REJECT vs ACCEPT; silent acceptance or silent record-drop is a conformance bug

evidence.quote: Bit 0x4 is the only reliable place to tell whether the read is unmapped. If 0x4 is set, no assumptions can be made about RNAME, POS, CIGAR, MAPQ, and bits 0x2, 0x100, and 0x800.

ambiguity_flags: The TLEN sign consistency rule is implied by paired-read semantics but not explicitly stated as MUST in the retrieved chunks

quarantine.mr_name: Coordinate boundary invariance under header reordering
quarantine.oracle: Coordinate-related API methods must return identical values after reordering @SQ header lines, as coordinate positions are independent of header line order

evidence.quote: [1-based coordinate system] A coordinate system where the first base of a sequence is one. In this coordinate system, a region is specified by a closed interval. For example, the region between the 3rd and the 7th bases inclusive is $[3,7]$. The SAM, VCF, GFF and Wiggle formats are using the 1-based coordinate system.

[SAM v1 — Indexing BAM] Indexing aims to achieve fast retrieval of alignments overlapping a specified region without going through the whole alignments. BAM must be sorted by the reference ID and then the leftmost coordinate before indexing.

evidence.quote:
description
[lexicographical] sort order is defined as a character-based dictionary sort with the character order as defined by the POSIX C locale.
  For example ``abc'', ``abc17'', ``abc5'', ``abc59'' and ``abcd'' are in lexicographical order.

quarantine.mr_name: SAM writer round-trip invariance
quarantine.oracle: parse(SAM) ≡ parse(write(parse(SAM)))

evidence.quote: Optional alignment fields are stored immediately after each other immediately following the qual field, and are included in block_size. Each field is represented as a two-character tag followed by a single type character and then its value, whose length is determined by the field's type.
